headline: G MINING VENTURES Acquires G2 Goldfields for C$3 Billion — 500K oz/yr Guyana Gold Hub Emerges
snippet: G Mining Ventures (GMIN) agreed to acquire G2 Goldfields in an all-stock deal valued at US$2.13B, combining the adjacent Oko West and Oko-Ghanie deposits in Guyana into a single district-scale project targeting 500,000+ oz/yr gold production and unlocking C$1B+ in synergies.

headline: GOLD HITS $4,761 — April Surge Signals Deepening Anxiety Over Fiscal and Monetary Stability
snippet: Gold rose to $4,761.42/oz on April 14, up 46% year-over-year, as investors treat the metal as a refuge from mounting fiscal deficits, central bank money expansion, and escalating geopolitical tensions across multiple theatres.

headline: CHINA LOCKS DOWN Rare Earths — New Export Controls on Medium & Heavy REEs Threaten Western Defense and EV Supply Chains
snippet: China's Announcement 18 imposes sweeping export licensing controls on medium and heavy rare earths as a national security measure. With China controlling 94% of global magnet manufacturing, the move could halt automotive, defense, and EV production lines in Western economies.

headline: COPPER Surges to $6.01/lb ($13,250/tonne) — Tariff Fears and Structural Supply Crunch Drive Records
snippet: Copper hit $6.01/lb on April 14 — up 30.6% year-over-year — as tariff uncertainty restricts trade flows and structural supply constraints remain unresolved. LME warehouse inventories climbed to an 8-year high, adding a cautionary signal to the otherwise bullish price action.

headline: Dryden Gold DRILLS 4.25m @ 32.87 g/t — Three New High-Grade Zones Discovered at Ontario's Gold Rock
snippet: Dryden Gold (TSX-V: DRY) hit three new zones in the Big Master Gold System at Gold Rock: 4.25m @ 32.87 g/t (peak 252 g/t), 3.80m @ 13.08 g/t, and 4.00m @ 6.51 g/t — all 300-metre step-outs from previously known high-grade intercepts at Treasure and Barrelman.

headline: Silver Set for SIXTH Straight Annual Supply Deficit — 67 Million-Oz Shortfall Projected for 2026
snippet: The Silver Institute projects a 67 million-oz structural deficit in 2026 as solar PV, EV production, AI data centres, and 5G infrastructure drive industrial demand above mine supply and recycling combined — the sixth consecutive year of shortfall.

headline: Core Lithium GREENLIGHTS Finniss Restart — A$290M Secured, Ore Mining Begins May 2026
snippet: Core Lithium approved the final investment decision to restart its Finniss lithium operation in the Northern Territory after securing A$290M in financing. Ore mining at the Grants pit begins May 2026, with first spodumene concentrate shipment targeted for Q3 2026.

headline: Denison Mines Gets FINAL CNSC Approval — Phoenix ISR Is Canada's First New Uranium Mine Approved in 20+ Years
snippet: Canada's CNSC granted final regulatory approval for Denison's Phoenix ISR uranium project at Wheeler River, Saskatchewan — the first large-scale Canadian uranium mine approved for construction in over two decades. Site preparation has commenced; first production is targeted for mid-2028.

headline: Trump Takes 10% EQUITY STAKE in USA Rare Earth — $1.6 Billion Bet on Domestic Rare Earth Independence
snippet: The US government agreed to invest $1.6B and take a 10% equity stake in USA Rare Earth to accelerate domestic mine development and magnet production, a direct counter to China's tightening grip on the rare earth supply chain.

headline: Gold-Silver Ratio CONTRACTS to 57:1 — Historical Extreme Reversal Points to Sharp Silver Outperformance Ahead
snippet: The gold-to-silver ratio peaked above 105 in April 2025 and has compressed to ~57:1 today — a nearly 45-point reversal. Every prior compression from extreme levels has preceded sharp silver outperformance, with silver already up 150% over the past year.

headline: Junior Miners TAKE CENTER STAGE — Record Gold Prices Trigger the Biggest Exploration Boom in a Decade
snippet: With gold up nearly 100% from cycle lows, junior mining exploration budgets are surging across the TSX-V and ASX. Drill rigs are booked months ahead, geologists are back in the field, and high-grade discoveries are drawing institutional capital back to the sector.

headline: AI Boom Set to TURBOCHARGE Uranium Demand — Data Centre Power Surge Forces Nuclear Back Into the Spotlight
snippet: Artificial intelligence data centres are emerging as the most significant new driver of global electricity demand, with nuclear widely viewed as the only baseload solution that can scale fast enough — tightening the already undersupplied uranium market further.
